name: Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga
name_original: Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular desde La Pesga
region_intro: Jogging around Hervas offers diverse terrain through the picturesque Ambroz Valley. The region is characterized by lush chestnut forests, rolling hills, and river valleys, providing varied running routes. Runners can explore former railway lines transformed into greenways, offering gentle slopes and scenic views, or opt for trails alongside the Ambroz River. The natural features and elevation changes make Hervas an engaging destination for running routes. Best jogging routes around Hervas The most popular jogging route is Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco, a 1.6 miles (2.6 km) trail that takes about 21 minutes to complete. This route follows the Ambroz River, leading to a waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga, a difficult 7.9 miles (12.6 km) path. This route offers expansive views from higher elevations. Local runners also love the CASTAÑAR GALLEGO HERVAS loop from Hervás, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through extensive chestnut forests, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Jogging around Hervas is defined by extensive chestnut forests, scenic river valleys, and converted greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle paths to more challenging hill climbs. The routes in Hervas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Hervas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Hervás?
A: Hervás offers a diverse selection of over 20 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. These trails wind through the picturesque Ambroz Valley, featuring lush chestnut forests, scenic river valleys, and converted greenways.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for jogging routes in Hervás?
A: The running routes around Hervás range from moderate to difficult. There are 11 moderate trails and 9 difficult trails, providing options for both intermediate runners and those seeking a more challenging experience with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls or river views?
A: Yes, Hervás is known for its beautiful river landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991203199/chorro-de-la-meancera-desde-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; route is a popular option that follows the Ambroz River and leads to an impressive waterfall. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991227952/rio-de-los-angeles-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río de los Ángeles River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, also offers scenic river views.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Hervás?
A: Absolutely. Many trails around Hervás are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990941014/mirador-del-pico-blanco-circular-from-la-pesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991224462/la-muela-and-la-cruz-de-jeromo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muela and La Cruz de Jeromo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CASTAÑAR GALLEGO HERVAS loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while running in Hervás?
A: Hervás offers stunning natural beauty. You can run through extensive chestnut forests, particularly on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CASTAÑAR GALLEGO HERVAS loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes provide views of the Ambroz Valley, and you might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4386882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Blanco Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive vistas.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Hervás?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural trails in Hervás are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. The Vía Verde de la Plata, a converted railway line, is often a good option for running with dogs due to its gentle slopes and open spaces. Always ensure to clean up after your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Hervás?
A: The mild weather during spring (April to June) and fall (September to November) makes these seasons ideal for jogging in Hervás. The natural scenery is particularly beautiful during these times, with lush greenery in spring and vibrant colors in autumn.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990941014/mirador-del-pico-blanco-circular-from-la-pesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically known for its expansive views from higher elevations. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; highlight along some trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Hervás?
A: The running routes in Hervás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Over 100 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the extensive chestnut forests, scenic river valleys, and the diverse options available for different ability levels.

Q: Is there public transport access to the running trails in Hervás?
A: Hervás is a small town, and while some trails are accessible directly from the town center, public transport options to specific trailheads outside the immediate vicinity might be limited. It's often best to check local bus schedules or consider driving to trail starting points, especially for routes further afield like those along the Vía Verde de la Plata.

Q: Are there any permits required for jogging on trails in Hervás?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for jogging on the established trails and greenways around Hervás, such as the Vía Verde de la Plata or routes through the Chestnut Forest. These areas are open for public enjoyment. However, always respect local signage and any specific regulations that might be posted in protected natural areas.

Q: What is the Vía Verde de la Plata and is it suitable for running?
A: The Vía Verde de la Plata is a former railway line transformed into a greenway, making it an excellent and accessible route for running. It features gentle slopes, open curves, and offers scenic views of the Sierra de Béjar and the Ambroz Valley. The 8-kilometer section connecting Baños de Montemayor with Hervás is particularly popular for its smooth surface and picturesque surroundings.
tips: The Mirador Pico Blanco in La Pesga, Extremadura, is a spectacular place to enjoy panoramic views. Located at the highest point in the area, it offers stunning views to the north of Las Hurdes and to the south of the Gabriel y Galán reservoir, the lands of Granadilla and the abandoned town of Granadilla.
